untranslatable_reason: Signal requires covariance matrix calculation, eigenvector decomposition, and spread (y3) normalization—operations not expressible using allowed indicators. The strategy uses multivariate statistical methods (PCA/cointegration) to construct a stationary portfolio and position sizing based on eigenvector weights and normalized spreads. These require external preprocessing outside the backtest engine's indicator vocabulary.
translation_notes: CHAN_AT_DRY_3 is a pairs/triplet cointegration strategy using eigenvector weighting for mean reversion. Core logic: (1) compute covariance of EWA/EWC/IGE, (2) extract first eigenvector for stationary weights, (3) construct normalized spread y3, (4) size positions = numUnits × eigenvector_weights × y3, (5) exit on mean reversion. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because long/short depends on spread sign and eigenvector orientation. The signal cannot be reduced to simple indicator conditions—it requires matrix operations and external spread calculation.
